topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The accusation landed like a physical blow. I stared at the printed page, the highlighted sections mocking me with their perfect, unearned prose. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded with a sudden, hot tension. "This is unacceptable," I managed, my voice thin and brittle. I didn't look at the professor; instead, I focused on the dust motes dancing in the afternoon light slicing through the window. Every word they’d written, every carefully constructed sentence, felt like a deliberate insult aimed squarely at my intelligence. I felt a deep, cold settling in my stomach, a quiet, simmering resentment that refused to dissipate. I gathered my books with deliberate, slow movements, the sound of the closing covers echoing in the suddenly silent room.

[story 2]

"You simply cannot believe this," I said, the words tasting like ash in my mouth. My hands clenched into fists inside my pockets, the knuckles turning white. I watched the committee members nod, their expressions a mixture of pity and judgment. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped me, heavy and controlled. Inside, a distinct feeling of being fundamentally misunderstood washed over me, a profound sense that they all viewed me as some sort of fraud. I straightened my posture until I felt rigid, every line of my spine protesting the humiliation. When the professor finally spoke, their tone was measured, which only amplified the feeling that they were treating me like a child who had broken a vase.

[story 3]

I picked up the notification email, the subject line a stark declaration of my supposed transgression. A tight, almost painful smile touched my lips before I forced it away. It was a gesture of utter disbelief, a silent acknowledgment of how absurd the situation was. I leaned back in my chair, deliberately creating distance between myself and the desk, allowing the silence to stretch taut between us. My gaze drifted to the framed diplomas on the wall—symbols of achievement that felt suddenly hollow. A slow, almost imperceptible shake of my head confirmed my internal assessment: this entire proceeding was an exercise in misplaced authority, a clumsy attempt to diminish something genuine. I simply waited, letting the weight of their expectations settle heavily upon the room.
